title_slide: How I Turned My Habits into an Engine to Get Me to My Goals

slides.heading: The Problem: Habits in Isolation
bullet_points: Habits in isolation = activity, not momentum
You can meditate, read, exercise — and still feel stuck
The gap between routine and engine is architecture
slides.speaker_note: Show the disconnect: consistent habits but delayed big goals. This is relatable to anyone who tracks everything but feels stalled.

slides.heading: The Core Shift
bullet_points: From: Did I do the habit?
To: Did this habit feed my bigger goal?
The feedback loop is where the magic happens
slides.speaker_note: This is the pivot. Show how reframing the question changes everything. Architecture does the work, not willpower.

slides.heading: The Three Categories
bullet_points: Foundation: sleep, movement, eating (baseline)
Work: deep focus, iteration (moves the goal forward)
Reflection: journaling, review (makes visible what works)
slides.speaker_note: Introduce the structure. Foundation supports work, work feeds reflection, reflection informs the next cycle.

slides.heading: Building the Links
bullet_points: Intention linking: write how foundation supports work each week
Feedback loops: ask weekly which habit enabled your best work
Ruthless deletion: does this serve a goal or support another habit?
slides.speaker_note: These are the concrete tools. This is the thinking part — it takes time but pays off in compounding returns.

slides.heading: The Discipline Myth
bullet_points: Well-built engine requires LESS discipline, not more
You maintain it because it works, not because you force yourself
The setup phase is hard. The maintenance phase feels like play.
slides.speaker_note: Counter the narrative about systems requiring iron discipline. Clarity and connection matter more than willpower.

slides.heading: Iteration and Evolution
bullet_points: An engine isn't built once — it's evolved
Mine looks nothing like it did a year ago
You need seasons of running to know what actually works
slides.speaker_note: This isn't a 30-day system. Real iteration takes time. That's what makes it powerful — it becomes yours.

slides.heading: The Real Payoff
bullet_points: Stewardship of something that works feels like play
Momentum is infrastructure, not motivation
Structure makes certain outcomes more likely
slides.speaker_note: Close with the outcome. Once the engine is built, the system does the heavy lifting. You're not pushing harder.
